# Student Loan Default - Consequences & Solutions [2026]
> Last updated: 2026-05-01

> What happens when you default on student loans? Garnishment, tax offsets, credit damage, and how to get out of default. 2026 guide.

## About
studentloandefault.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Consolidation After Student Loan Default - Fast Exit From Default: https://studentloandefault.org/consolidation-after-default.html
- Credit Impact of Student Loan Default - How Bad Is It?: https://studentloandefault.org/credit-impact-default.html
- Student Loan Default Timeline - What Happens Month by Month: https://studentloandefault.org/default-timeline.html
- Getting Out of Student Loan Default - All Your Options: https://studentloandefault.org/getting-out-of-default.html
- Student Loan Default - Consequences & Solutions: https://studentloandefault.org/
- Private Student Loan Default - Different Rules, Different Risks: https://studentloandefault.org/private-loan-default.html
- Student Loan Rehabilitation - Remove Default From Your Credit Report: https://studentloandefault.org/rehabilitation.html
- Student Loan Social Security Offset - Protecting Benefits From Seizure: https://studentloandefault.org/social-security-offset.html
- Student Loan Tax Refund Offset - When the IRS Takes Your Refund: https://studentloandefault.org/tax-refund-offset.html
- Student Loan Wage Garnishment - How Much They Can Take and How to Stop It: https://studentloandefault.org/wage-garnishment.html
- Privacy Policy: https://studentloandefault.org/privacy.html

## Contact
- Website: https://openbankruptcyproject.org
- Email: contact@openbankruptcyproject.org
- GitHub: https://github.com/openbankruptcyproject

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
